geologicalEpochs.name: Hadean
geologicalEpochs.description: Molten Earth, Moon formation, first crust
geologicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: Earth accretes, no rocks from this period survive

geologicalEpochs.name: Proterozoic
geologicalEpochs.description: Great Oxidation Event, Snowball Earth, first eukaryotes
geologicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: Basement rocks may underlie area at depth

geologicalEpochs.name: Cambrian
geologicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: No Five Towns outcrops — area beneath sea

geologicalEpochs.name: Carboniferous
geologicalEpochs.description: Coal Age — Yorkshire Coal Measures deposited
geologicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: ⭐ PRIMARY: Coal Measures underlie Five Towns at depth

geologicalEpochs.name: Permian
geologicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: ⭐ Yorkshire Magnesian Limestone surface geology east of Five Towns

geologicalEpochs.name: Jurassic
geologicalEpochs.description: Dinosaur era, Yorkshire coast limestone and clays
geologicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: ⭐ Jurassic Oxfordian limestone — dinosaur fossils at Whitby, Scarborough

geologicalEpochs.name: Quaternary
geologicalEpochs.description: Ice Age — Pleistocene glaciations, Devensian, Holocene
geologicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: ⭐ Devensian glaciation deposited Yorkshire till; post-glacial peat

politicalEpochs.name: Roman Britain
politicalEpochs.description: Roman conquest 43 AD, roads, forts, 400-year occupation
politicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: ⭐ Roman Causennae at Pontefract — Roman road passes through

politicalEpochs.name: Norman-Medieval
politicalEpochs.description: Norman Conquest, feudal system, castles, monasteries

politicalEpochs.name: Victorian
politicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: ⭐ Victorian: Coal mines at peak; Featherstone, Knottingley, Pontefract; railways connect all five towns

politicalEpochs.name: Modern
politicalEpochs.fiveTownsRelevance: 1984 miners' strike: Featherstone violence; pit closures; economic transformation
